message:
feat(extensions): unified capability policy model with per-extension overrides

Introduce a comprehensive extension policy system that replaces the
flat allow/deny approach with a layered, configurable architecture:

Capability taxonomy (src/extensions.rs):
- New Capability enum with 10 typed variants (read, write, http, events,
  session, ui, exec, env, tool, log) replacing raw string comparisons
- is_dangerous() classification for exec/env capabilities
- Roundtrip-safe parsing with case-insensitive matching

Policy profiles (src/extensions.rs):
- PolicyProfile presets (Safe, Standard, Permissive) providing curated
  defaults — Safe denies dangerous caps in Strict mode, Permissive
  allows everything
- Per-extension overrides via ExtensionOverride struct with allow/deny
  lists and optional mode override

5-layer precedence chain for evaluate_for():
  1. Per-extension deny → Deny
  2. Global deny_caps → Deny
  3. Per-extension allow → Allow
  4. Global default_caps → Allow
  5. Mode fallback (Strict→Deny, Prompt→Prompt, Permissive→Allow)

Configuration (src/config.rs):
- ExtensionPolicyConfig struct with profile + allow_dangerous fields
- resolve_extension_policy() with precedence: CLI flag > env var
  (PI_EXTENSION_POLICY) > settings.json > default
- Additional serde aliases (camelCase) for defaultProvider, defaultModel,
  shellPath, thinkingBudgets, etc. for JSON config compat
- Graceful handling of empty settings.json files

CLI integration (src/cli.rs, src/main.rs):
- --extension-policy flag accepting safe/standard/permissive profiles
- Wired into startup via enable_extensions_with_policy()

Agent plumbing (src/agent.rs):
- New enable_extensions_with_policy() method that accepts an optional
  ExtensionPolicy, forwarding to both JS and WASM extension hosts
- Original enable_extensions() preserved as backward-compatible wrapper

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
